definition.name: unital_star_algebra
definition.definition: A unital *-algebra A is a complex associative algebra equipped with (i) an identity element 1 ∈ A (unitality), and (ii) an antilinear involution * : A → A satisfying (ab)* = b*a* and (a*)* = a for all a, b ∈ A. An element a ∈ A is called self-adjoint if a* = a; the real subspace of self-adjoint elements is denoted A^{sa}. When A is additionally equipped with a C*-norm (i.e., a complete submultiplicative norm satisfying ||a*a|| = ||a||²), it is a C*-algebra. Standard examples: the matrix algebra M_n(C), the bounded operators B(H) on a Hilbert space, continuous functions C(X) on a compact space (with pointwise conjugation), and group C*-algebras C*(Γ). Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.1.
